generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotorFirstAxis : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor for the first axis of a robotic system (MABI MAX 100). Directly influenced by current input (I_M) to produce torque output (τ_M). Operates under closed-loop control via Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller with torque-speed characteristic limitations."
]

instance SiemensSINUMERIK840DslController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller managing remote actuation and precise motion execution for the robotic system. Implements closed-loop feedback mechanisms using encoder-based position feedback."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorShaftPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-resolution motor encoder mounted directly on the motor shaft providing angular position data for closed-loop control and friction loss detection."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorShaftPositionDataTransmitted
]

instance GearboxPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Secondary position encoder positioned behind the gearbox providing redundant angular position data with sub-millimeter/sub-degree resolution for dynamic simulations."
    DTDFVocab:producedData GearboxPositionDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance MotorShaftPositionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorShaftPositionEncoder
    base:desc "Real-time angular position data from motor shaft encoder with high resolution for friction modeling and digital shadow traceability."
]

instance GearboxPositionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GearboxPositionEncoder
    base:desc "Redundant angular position data from gearbox encoder ensuring accuracy for dynamic simulations and closed-loop control validation."
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance RoboticSystemGeometricModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "CAD-based geometric model of the MABI MAX 100 serial robot focusing on first axis kinematics and mechatronic components."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingFramework, MontiArcADL
]

instance FrictionCompensationBehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based behavioral model simulating friction forces in the first axis using state machines (SysML v1.7 or MontiArc Automaton) for real-time compensation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MathWorksSimulink, CppImplementation
]

instance TorqueSpeedCharacteristicAnalyticalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Mathematical model representing the torque-speed characteristics of the permanent magnet synchronous motor for control system validation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MathWorksSimulink, JavaImplementation
]

instance MotorShaftPositionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated high-resolution angular position data from motor shaft encoder for digital shadow creation and friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorShaftPositionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DigitalShadowReferenceModel, SysMLModelingFramework
]

instance GearboxPositionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Redundant position data from gearbox encoder ensuring accuracy for dynamic simulations and closed-loop control validation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData GearboxPositionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DigitalShadowReferenceModel, MathWorksSimulink
]

instance ControllerFeedbackData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed feedback data from Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller including position, velocity, and torque values for real-time synchronization."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorShaftPositionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MathWorksSimulink, CppImplementation
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLModelingFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML-based framework for modeling system structure and behavior across abstraction layers (requirements, functional, logical, and product architectures)."
    DTDFVocab:enables RoboticSystemMonitoringService, FrictionAnalysisService
]

instance MontiArcADL : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MontiArc Architecture Description Language for component-based system modeling including interfaces, connectors, and behavioral descriptions using MontiArc Automaton."
    DTDFVocab:enables MotionControlOptimizationService
]

instance MathWorksSimulink : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Simulation and behavioral modeling tool for detailed dynamic analysis of mechatronic systems including friction compensation and control system validation."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveMaintenanceService, RealTimeControlService
]

instance DigitalShadowReferenceModel : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Framework defining passive data collection (one-way flow) from physical objects to digital representations with aggregation and abstraction of contextual data traces."
    DTDFVocab:enables DigitalShadowCreationService
]

instance CppImplementation : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "General-purpose implementation language for system logic including control algorithms and real-time processing components."
    DTDFVocab:enables MotionControlExecutionService, SafetyMonitoringService
]

instance JavaImplementation : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "General-purpose implementation language for system integration and data processing components."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataAggregationService, SystemIntegrationService
]

// C6: Services
instance RoboticSystemMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing real-time monitoring of robotic system state including position, velocity, and torque metrics for operational oversight."
    DTDFVocab:provides RoboticSystemStateInsight
]

instance FrictionAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service analyzing friction forces in the first axis using digital shadow data and behavioral models for predictive compensation."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionForceInsight, FrictionCompensationAction
]

instance MotionControlOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service optimizing motion control parameters based on real-time feedback and analytical models to improve system performance."
    DTDFVocab:provides MotionOptimizationInsight, ParameterAdjustmentAction
]

instance DigitalShadowCreationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service creating and maintaining digital shadow representations of physical components with contextual data traces for analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides DigitalShadowAccuracyInsight
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service predicting maintenance needs based on friction analysis, wear patterns, and operational data to prevent failures."
    DTDFVocab:provides MaintenancePredictionInsight, MaintenanceScheduleAction
]

instance RealTimeControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [

    DTDFVocab:provides ControlStabilityInsight
]

instance MotionControlExecutionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service executing motion control commands through the Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller with closed-loop feedback."
    DTDFVocab:provides MotionExecutionInsight
]

instance SafetyMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [

    DTDFVocab:provides SafetyConditionInsight, EmergencyStopAction
]

instance DataAggregationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service aggregating and preprocessing raw sensor data for digital shadow integration and analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataQualityInsight
]

instance SystemIntegrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service integrating heterogeneous data sources and models across the digital twin constellation."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemIntegrationInsight
]

]

]

]

]

// C1: Environment
instance RoboticSystemFirstAxisEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotorFirstAxis,
                SiemensSINUMERIK840DslController,
                MotorShaftPositionEncoder,
                GearboxPositionEncoder

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system incorporates **validation and verification** mechanisms to ensure model fidelity, focusing on **comparing simulated data against measured data** to detect deviations (e.g., friction loss in manufacturing processes). **Traceability** is enforced via the *originates from* association in the digital shadow reference model, enabling retrospective analysis of data traces back to their generating processing components, consumed inputs, and involved models. This supports **quality assurance** by linking data provenance to model accuracy, though explicit methods for uncertainty quantification, formal verification, or calibration techniques are not detailed in the provided context."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The document briefly mentions the use of **MQTT** as a communication protocol for streaming data in the context of Digital Shadows (Section IV-A, Step 4). However, no further details are provided regarding **network topology**, **architecture**, **bandwidth/latency requirements**, **reliability/redundancy mechanisms**, or **security protocols** for network communication. The reference to MQTT implies a lightweight, publish-subscribe model for data transmission, but no technical specifications (e.g., QoS levels, encryption methods, or failover strategies) are described."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Shadow system integrates with external data sources by streaming sensor data (input current and output angle) from the physical robot axis via the **MQTT** protocol, enabling real-time data acquisition for friction modeling. No explicit mention of integration with other Digital Twins, enterprise systems, or standardized interoperability protocols beyond MQTT for sensor data streaming is provided in the document."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** standards, including the use of **SysML** (Systems Modeling Language) for system modeling across abstraction layers. **Architecture Description Languages (ADLs)** such as **ACME**, **AADL**, **MontiArc**, and **SysML IBDs** are standardized for component-based system descriptions, with behavioral extensions like **MontiArc Automaton** or **SysML state machines** (v1.7+) ensuring compliance with formal modeling practices. Interoperability is addressed through integration with **simulation tools** (e.g., **MathWorks Simulink**) and **general-purpose programming languages** (e.g., **C++**, **Java**), though no specific interoperability standards (e.g., OPC UA, FIWARE) are mentioned. The context does not specify **certification requirements** or **compliance frameworks**, but the reliance on standardized modeling languages implies adherence to industry best practices for system engineering. **Digital Shadow Reference Model** (as per Kritzinger et al.) defines foundational concepts but does not explicitly outline broader standardization frameworks."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Shadow system for the MABI MAX 100 serial robot incorporates **safety-critical constraints** enforced by a **safety cell**, which restricts the operational range of the first vertical axis to **[-90°, 90°]** and limits the maximum achievable angular speed to **|q̇_max| = 75 [°/s]**. The system leverages **dual position encoders**—one mounted directly on the motor and another behind the gearbox—to mitigate **backlash** and **positional uncertainty** by analyzing encoder discrepancies to infer **elasticity and friction** in the drivetrain. The **Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller** ensures **closed-loop control** of the axis, with inputs including position, speed, or acceleration, and outputs comprising target/actual position, derivatives, and motor current. The motor current is converted to torque using **known motor constants**, enabling real-time torque monitoring. No explicit cybersecurity protocols, authentication systems, or fail-safe mechanisms beyond mechanical and control-system safeguards are documented."
]
